title: thymus serpyllum subsp. ovatus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. ovatus, also known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading evergreen herb native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is known for its small, fragrant leaves and pink or white flowers, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of creeping thyme have been found to provide various health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help to reduce stress and promote overall well-being. Creeping thyme can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
